01 · About

I don't just build dashboards.
I build clarity.

I'm a data analyst who believes the best analysis is invisible - when a dashboard is so clear that the decision becomes obvious, the work is done right.

At Unseen UK, I automated reporting workflows that previously ate hours of manual effort every week. The result wasn't just 20% faster delivery - it was a team that finally had time to act on the data rather than just collect it.

My background bridges two worlds: rigorous academic training (MSc, Aston University) and real business environments where accuracy isn't negotiable and stakeholders don't have time for caveats.

Dubai's data landscape is exactly what excites me - fast-moving, high-stakes, and hungry for people who can translate numbers into decisions. I'm ready from day one.

English (Full Professional) · Malayalam (Native) · Tamil (Working)

03 · Flagship Case Study

Retail Intelligence.
Full-stack analysis.

★ Flagship Project · End-to-End Case Study

Full-Stack
Retail Revenue Analysis

UK Retail · 2024 · SQL + Python + Power BI
SQL Python Power BI Pandas Matplotlib DAX Jupyter Notebook Excel
500K+
Transactions
£8M+
Revenue Analysed
Top 20%
Customer Segment

A UAE-focused online retailer operating across 38 international markets lacked visibility into customer lifecycle behaviour, purchase funnel leakage, seasonal demand concentration, and international growth opportunities despite generating over £10M annual revenue.

Executed a full-stack analytics workflow covering Excel data preparation, Python-based RFM clustering, cohort retention analysis, funnel analytics, revenue forecasting, MySQL window-function querying, and Power BI dashboard development with KPI validation layers.

Designed a 3-page executive Power BI dashboard integrating Python and SQL outputs through structured auto-refresh pipelines. Built validation-layer DAX measures enabling stakeholders to analyse customer intelligence, retention trends, product performance, and geographic revenue patterns interactively.

Python MySQL Power BI DAX RFM Segmentation Cohort Analysis Time Series
View on GitHub ↗
Key Findings
01
Guest customers generated £1.74M revenue despite lacking CRM identification, revealing a major customer acquisition and retention opportunity.
02
Belgium, Germany, and France outperformed the UK on repeat purchase conversion rates, signalling stronger international retention potential.
03
Wholesale and B2B Champion customers averaged £125K spend, behaving distinctly from standard retail customer segments.
04
Forecasting models achieved R² = 0.894, confirming strong predictability in seasonal purchasing behaviour ahead of Q4 demand peaks.
Business Recommendations
Implement checkout email-capture incentives to convert high-value guest buyers into trackable CRM customers. Expand retention investment toward high-performing international markets and separate wholesale accounts from retail lifecycle strategies. Use forecasting outputs to support pre-Q4 procurement planning, inventory allocation, and supplier negotiations ahead of peak seasonal demand.
04 · Other Projects

Real data.
Real insight.

↗
01 · 2025
UAE Real Estate

Dubai Property Market Analysis

Excel · Pivot Tables · EDA · UAE Market
Property Market EDA · Excel
Problem Dubai’s residential property market across 54 neighbourhoods lacked structured pricing intelligence, quality-tier segmentation, and neighbourhood benchmarking for investor decision-making.
Method Performed end-to-end property analytics across 1,905 listings using price-per-sqft benchmarking, quality-tier segmentation, amenity analysis, and price-band modelling.
Finding Jumeirah recorded the highest price per sqft (AED 2,174), while the AED 1M–2M segment showed the strongest market liquidity with 767 listings.
Recommendation Target AED 1M–2M investments in Dubai Marina, JVC, and Business Bay where demand and liquidity overlap is strongest.
1,905
Listings
54
Neighbourhoods
Excel EDA Property Analytics UAE Market
02 · 2025
Retail Intelligence

Retail Intelligence & Revenue Analytics

SQL · Python · Power BI · DAX · Pandas · EDA
Problem Revenue of £10.24M across 522K transactions lacked customer-level visibility, retention tracking, and forecasting capability for commercial decision-making.
Method Excel governance → Python RFM segmentation & cohort analysis → MySQL window functions → Power BI DAX dashboards across 3 reporting pages.
Finding Top 20% of customers generated 68% of revenue. Q4 contributed 44.4% of annual sales, while 69.4% of repeat buyers never progressed into high-value segments.
Recommendation Deploy targeted retention and win-back campaigns while prioritising Q4 inventory planning and high-value customer management.

03 · 2023
Forecasting · ML

73-Year Global Population Forecast

Python · Scikit-learn · Pandas · UN Data
73yr Forecast · ML Model
Problem Population growth trends across India, China, UK, USA, and Brazil lacked a unified framework for long-term demographic forecasting.
Method Built a forecasting pipeline using UN demographic data, exploratory analysis, and predictive modelling with Linear Regression, ARIMA, Gradient Boosting, Exponential Smoothing, and Prophet.
Finding Linear Regression achieved R² = 0.999 across all five countries, while Prophet delivered the strongest long-term forecasting performance.
Recommendation Prioritise Prophet for long-term forecasting and extend models with migration, fertility, and economic indicators for improved demographic planning.
73yr
Data Span
5
Countries
Python Scikit-learn Forecasting UN Data Time Series

Data tells a story. I make it speak.
★ Retail Intelligence · Full Case Study
Retail Performance
Dashboard & Analysis
UK ONLINE RETAIL · FY 2011 · SQL + PYTHON + POWER BI
£9.47M
Total Revenue
18,225
Total Orders
4,215
Unique Customers
£520
Avg Order Value
64.1%
Repeat Purchase Rate
1,625
Champion Customers
Click to fullscreen ↗
Key Findings
01
Revenue peaked at £1.47M in November - a 31.6% MoM spike driven by gifting season, while Feb–Apr consistently underperformed at under £600K.
02
UK accounts for 91.1% of revenue - a critical concentration risk. Netherlands (3.1%) and Germany (2.2%) show growth potential but remain untapped.
03
Overall MoM growth averaged +5.2% across FY2011, but December saw a sharp –50% drop, indicating heavy post-Christmas cancellations.
04
Customer segment mix shows 39.7% Champions dominating revenue - but 23.4% are At Risk, requiring immediate retention action.
01
1,625 Champions generate over £6M in revenue - the highest of all segments, yet no VIP loyalty mechanism exists to protect this group.
02
Repeat purchase rate is 64.1% but 2,829 customers have only bought twice - a targeted nudge within 30 days could move them into Loyal Customers.
03
704 churn-risk customers identified - improving despite high count. Personalised win-back campaigns targeting these customers could recover £180K+ before FY2012.
04
Avg Customer LTV is £2,246, up 10.9% MoM - indicating that retained customers spend progressively more over time.
01
Top 10 products account for disproportionate revenue share - the highest-selling SKU alone drives 3× the revenue of the 10th-ranked product.
02
Product affinity analysis reveals strong cross-sell clusters - customers buying Category A have a 67% probability of also purchasing Category C within 30 days.
03
Over 40% of SKUs contributed less than 1% of total revenue - indicating significant catalogue bloat that increases fulfilment cost without revenue return.
04
Seasonal products show revenue spikes of 200–400% in Q4 - no current stocking model accounts for this, leading to stockouts at peak demand.
Business Recommendations
1
Replicate the November surge. Launch targeted Ramadan and Eid promotions to fill the Feb–Apr trough - these cultural moments align with gifting behaviour and could add £200K+ in off-peak revenue.
2
Reduce UK market dependency. Prioritise Netherlands and Germany for MENA expansion pilots - both show consistent repeat order patterns despite low volume.
3
Address December drop-off. Implement a post-purchase satisfaction check and flexible returns window to reduce cancellation rates and protect Q4 revenue.
Business Recommendations
1
Protect Champions immediately. Launch a VIP loyalty programme with early access and exclusive offers - losing even 10% of this segment costs over £600K in revenue.
2
Accelerate Repeat Purchases. Send personalised incentives to the 2,829 twice-buyers within 30 days - converting 20% to Loyal Customers adds ~£900K in LTV.
3
Win-back at-risk customers. Deploy personalised discount offers to the 704 churn-risk segment - a 25% recovery rate at average LTV returns approximately £180K before FY2012 close.
Business Recommendations
1
Build a cross-sell engine. Use affinity analysis clusters to trigger automated "frequently bought together" recommendations - estimated 8–12% basket size increase based on existing co-purchase patterns.
2
Rationalise the catalogue. Discontinue or bundle the bottom 40% of SKUs - freeing warehouse space and reducing fulfilment overhead while concentrating marketing on proven performers.
3
Build a seasonal stocking model. Use forecasting data to pre-position Q4 inventory 8 weeks earlier - eliminating the stockout gap that currently loses an estimated £300K+ in peak demand.
